title: Customer support chat: faster message delivery (dbg144)
description:
After dbg143 fixed bidirectional chat delivery, messages still arrived with long delays — often 10–20 seconds — especially on the admin side. Kat could see her own "lol" immediately (optimistic POST append) while Mike's chat stayed empty until much later.

Root cause (dbg144): dbg143 added GET /messages polling only inside the presence heartbeat, which runs every 20 seconds (HEARTBEAT_VISIBLE_MS). When SSE chat-message events were missed or slow (admin init deferred up to 3s, SSE reconnect at 3s, presence backoff skipping heartbeat entirely), the only recovery path was that 20s poll. Backend broadcast is synchronous and instant — latency was entirely client-side.

Fix: dedicated 2s chat poll while a session is live (independent of presence heartbeat/backoff). Start poll when chat goes active; stop on end. SSE reconnect reduced to 1.5s. Admin support init idle timeout cut from 3s to 500ms so SSE connects sooner.

Who is affected: Horizon admins and customers using PipeShare customer support chat on the OVH private server. Frontend-only deploy is sufficient.
changeLog: Poll GET /messages every 2s during active chat — no longer tied to 20s presence heartbeat.
Chat poll runs even when presence heartbeat is in backoff; removed sync from heartbeat.
Start/stop chat poll on session live/end; immediate poll on tab visible.
SSE reconnect retry 3s → 1.5s; admin support init idle timeout 3s → 500ms.
Cache bust dbg144 (portal runtime + PipeShare addon manifest + PipeSync manifest).
